sections.title: Payment transaction execution definition
sections.summary: This section defines payment transaction execution services as services that include direct debits, payment card transactions, and credit transfers. It specifies the types of payment transactions covered under the directive.

requirements.text: Execution of payment transactions, including transfers of funds on a payment account with the user's payment service provider or with another payment service provider

sections.source_quote: Execution of payment transactions, including transfers of funds on a payment account with the user's payment service provider or with another payment service provider: (a) execution of direct debits, including one-off direct debits; (b) execution of payment transactions through a payment card or a similar device; (c) execution of credit transfers, including standing orders.

sections.title: Execution of payment transactions using credit lines
sections.summary: This section specifies payment transaction execution methods that use credit lines for payment service users. It includes direct debits, payment cards, and credit transfers. These activities are covered under the payment service framework where funds are backed by credit lines.

sections.title: Transitional period for payment service providers
sections.summary: Member States cannot prohibit payment service providers that were active before January 12, 2016, from continuing their operations during a transitional period. This transitional period allows providers to maintain their services under existing regulatory frameworks until they comply with new standards.
sections.dtia_summary: This section ensures payment service providers can continue operations during a transitional period without being blocked from cross-border data transfers, which is critical for DTIA planning in the early stages of implementation.

requirements.text: Member States shall not forbid legal persons that have performed in their territories, before 12 January 2016, activities of payment initiation service providers and account information service providers

sections.title: Preventing payment service abuse during transition
sections.summary: Member States must prevent payment service providers from blocking or obstructing payment services for accounts they service if they are not yet compliant with regulatory technical standards. This ensures uninterrupted access to payment services during the transition period.
sections.dtia_summary: This section addresses the risk of payment service providers abusing non-compliance to disrupt services, which is critical for DTIA to ensure continuous data flow and service availability during regulatory transitions.

requirements.text: Member States shall ensure that until individual account servicing payment service providers comply with the regulatory technical standards referred to in paragraph 4

account servicing payment service providers do not abuse their non-compliance to block or obstruct the use of payment initiation and account information services for the accounts that they are servicing

sections.source_quote: Member States shall ensure that until individual account servicing payment service providers comply with the regulatory technical standards referred to in paragraph 4, account servicing payment service providers do not abuse their non-compliance to block or obstruct the use of payment initiation and account information services for the accounts that they are servicing.
